title: I Stopped Prompting AI One Task At A Time. This Works Better.
keyIdeas:
**2-sentence summary**  
The video introduces "loops" as recurring AI workflows with memory that handle real-world tasks (like school trip prep or grocery management), and "loop of loops" as interconnected loops that share context and reduce mental load by automating entire processes. The key shift is from prompting AI one task at a time to building remembered, self-organizing agents that bring you in only when judgment is needed.

**Key takeaways**  
- A **prompt** is a one-time request; a **loop** is a recurring job with memory; a **loop of loops** connects multiple loops so they hand off context and stop at your boundaries.  
- Most useful work is not a single question but a recurring situation (e.g., customer follow-up, school trip prep) that lives **between apps**, not inside any one app.  
- Loops organize your attention: you design the workflow once, and the AI runs it repeatedly, only “waking you up” when human judgment is required.  
- Practical examples include a trip loop that wakes packing, weather, schedule, and calendar loops; a grocery loop that reminds you when spinach will go bad; and a sales loop that tracks objections, follow-ups, and pricing questions.  
- To build a loop of loops, start with one tedious but low-risk process (e.g., filling out product use cases into tickets) so you can trust the automation without catastrophic failure.  
- The goal is to lift mental load: loops catch problems before they happen (e.g., knowing when to size up clothes for a growing child) rather than requiring you to manually connect information across tools.

**Notable quotes, numbers, or claims**  
- *“The loop lives between them. And for the last 20 years that's been on us.”* – describing how apps digitized pieces of life but made humans do the wiring.  
- *“99% of your mental effort”* is the difference between prompting and using loops.  
- *“If it's recurring, you can loop it.”* – a simple rule for identifying automation opportunities.  
- *“Don't do anything with banking for your initial loop of loops.”* – advice to start with low-stakes tasks.  
- The creator promises a follow-up video on Friday showing exactly how they built a loop of loops system.
